The Vedhya positions for Rahu and Ketu are 3, 6, and 11 as for Saturn  and the Vedha positions are 12, 9, 5.

chapters.content_preview: 2. Under the following circumstances, the enemy will not invade or even if the march has  begun, will abandon it: (a) The Moon is in a moveable sign and the Ascendant is in a fixed sign and a fixed  Navamsa — an invasion by the enemy need not be feared. chapters.content_preview: 3. When the enemy marches on against the querent, the outcome is decided in the  following manner: (a) The Ascendant is a fixed sign or the Moon is in a common sign and the 6th house is  occupied by Mercury, Jupiter or Venus — the invading army will be defeated. This particular square, which is in the S.E., indicates the  spot where water will be available. This diagram may be considered as a plan or a b

chapters.title: The base or Kuta number is 49.
chapters.content_preview: 7. The base or Kuta number is 49. Example: (a) Arudha Lagna = Scorpio. (b) The number assigned to it is 11. (c) Deducting 11 from the base number 49, we get 49 — 11 = 38. (d) Multiplying the balance by 7, we get 38 x 7 = 266. (e) Dividing the product by 27, we get 266 / 27 = 9 pl

chapters.title: The birth Lagna is the Navamsa sign of the query Lagna.
chapters.content_preview: 1. The birth Lagna is the Navamsa sign of the query Lagna. For instance if in the query chart the Navamsa Lagna is Leo, then in the birth chart the  Lagna will be Leo. 2. Take the Drekkana of the ascendant in the query chart. Count from it till the Drekkana  occupied by the Sun.

chapters.title: Take the Drekkana of the ascendant in the query chart. Count from it till the Drekkana
chapters.content_preview: 2. Take the Drekkana of the ascendant in the query chart. Count from it till the Drekkana  occupied by the Sun. Note the number of Drekkanas, expunge multiples of 12. The remainder  counted from the query ascendant gives the birth ascendant. Example: The 2nd Drekkana of Taurus ri
